revision.text:
'''十二直'''(じゅうにちょく)とは[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建'''・'''除'''・'''満'''・'''平'''・'''定'''・'''執'''・'''破'''・'''危'''・'''成'''・'''納'''・'''開'''・'''閉'''のことである。 暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。「直」には「当たる」という意味があり、よく当たる暦注だと信じられていたと考えられる。 ==由来== [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを吉凶判断に用いたのが'''十二直'''である。 昭和初期までは、'''十二直'''が暦注の中でも最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている。 ==配当== 柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分(斗柄)が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。 十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。[[節月]]ごとに、その月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には、[[節気]]の日に、その前の日の十二直を繰り返す。 冬至の頃には斗柄が北(子)を指す(建(おざ)す)ので、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。 ==運勢== それぞれの十二直の意味と、一般的に言われているその日の運勢を示す。なお、最近の運勢暦では、十二直はひらがなで書かれることが多い。
十二直 意味 運勢
建 たつ 万物を建て生じる日 よろず大吉の日。但し動土・蔵開きは凶
除 のぞく 障碍を取り除く日 井戸掘り・治療開始・祭祀などは吉。結婚・動土は凶
満 みつ 全てが満たされる日 新規事・移転・結婚などは吉。動土・服薬は凶
平 たいら 物事が平らかになる日 旅行・結婚・道路修理などは吉。穴堀り・種まきは凶
定 さだん 善悪が定まる日 開店・結婚・移転・種まきは吉。旅行・訴訟は凶
執 とる 執り行う日 祭祀・祝い事・造作・種まきは吉。金銭の出入りは凶
破 やぶる 物事を突破する日 訴訟・出陣・漁猟・服薬は吉。祝い事・契約事は凶
危 あやぶ 物事を危惧する日 万事控えめに
成 なる 物事が成就する日 新規事・建築・開店は吉。訴訟・談判は凶
納 おさん 物事を納め入れる日 収穫・商品購入は吉。結婚・見合いは凶
開 ひらく 開き通じる日 建築・移転・結婚等は吉。葬式は凶
閉 とづ 閉じ込める日 金銭出納・建墓は吉。棟上げ・結婚・開店は凶

破 やぶる 物事を突破する日 訴訟・出陣・漁猟・服薬は吉。祝い事・契約事は凶
危 あやぶ 物事を危惧する日 万事控えめに
成 なる 物事が成就する日 新規事・建築・開店は吉。訴訟・談判は凶

revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Twelve_Directions]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''十二直'''(じゅうにちょく)とは[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。 暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。「直」には「当たる」という意味があり、よく当たる暦注だと信じられていたと考えられる。 ==由来== [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを吉凶判断に用いたのが'''十二直'''である。 昭和初期までは、'''十二直'''が暦注の中でも最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている。 ==配当== 柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分(斗柄)が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。 十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月ごとに、その月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には、節月の始まりの日に、その前の日の十二直を繰り返す。 冬至の頃には斗柄が北(子)を指す(建(おざ)す)ので、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。 ==運勢== それぞれの十二直の意味と、一般的に言われているその日の運勢を示す。なお、最近の運勢暦では、十二直はひらがなで書かれることが多い。
十二直 意味 運勢
建 たつ 万物を建て生じる日 よろず大吉の日。但し動土・蔵開きは凶
除 のぞく 障碍を取り除く日 井戸掘り・治療開始・祭祀などは吉。結婚・動土は凶
満 みつ 全てが満たされる日 新規事・移転・結婚などは吉。動土・服薬は凶
平 たいら 物事が平らかになる日 旅行・結婚・道路修理などは吉。穴堀り・種まきは凶
定 さだん 善悪が定まる日 開店・結婚・移転・種まきは吉。旅行・訴訟は凶
執 とる 執り行う日 祭祀・祝い事・造作・種まきは吉。金銭の出入りは凶
破 やぶる 物事を突破する日 訴訟・出陣・漁猟・服薬は吉。祝い事・契約事は凶
危 あやぶ 物事を危惧する日 万事控えめに
成 なる 物事が成就する日 新規事・建築・開店は吉。訴訟・談判は凶
納 おさん 物事を納め入れる日 収穫・商品購入は吉。結婚・見合いは凶
開 ひらく 開き通じる日 建築・移転・結婚等は吉。葬式は凶
閉 とづ 閉じ込める日 金銭出納・建墓は吉。棟上げ・結婚・開店は凶

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} [[category:暦注|しゆうにちよく]] [[Category:名数12|ちよく]]

'''十二直'''(じゅうにちょく)とは[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。 暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。「直」には「当たる」という意味があり、よく当たる暦注だと信じられていたと考えられる。 ==由来== [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを吉凶判断に用いたのが'''十二直'''である。 昭和初期までは、'''十二直'''が暦注の中でも最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている。[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm 十二直について] ==配当== 柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分(斗柄)が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。 十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月ごとに、その月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には、節月の始まりの日に、その前の日の十二直を繰り返す。 冬至の頃には斗柄が北(子)を指す(建(おざ)す)ので、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。 ==運勢== それぞれの十二直の意味と、一般的に言われているその日の運勢を示す。なお、最近の運勢暦では、十二直はひらがなで書かれることが多い。
十二直 意味 運勢
建 たつ 万物を建て生じる日 よろず大吉の日。但し動土・蔵開きは凶
除 のぞく 障害を取り除く日 井戸掘り・治療開始・祭祀などは吉。結婚・動土は凶
満 みつ 全てが満たされる日 新規事・移転・結婚などは吉。動土・服薬は凶
平 たいら 物事が平らかになる日 旅行・結婚・道路修理などは吉。穴掘り・種まきは凶
定 さだん 善悪が定まる日 開店・結婚・移転・種まきは吉。旅行・訴訟は凶
執 とる 執り行う日 祭祀・祝い事・造作・種まきは吉。金銭の出入りは凶
破 やぶる 物事を突破する日 訴訟・出陣・漁猟・服薬は吉。祝い事・契約事は凶
危 あやぶ 物事を危惧する日 万事控えめに
成 なる 物事が成就する日 新規事・建築・開店は吉。訴訟・談判は凶
納 おさん 物事を納め入れる日 収穫・商品購入は吉。結婚・見合いは凶
開 ひらく 開き通じる日 建築・移転・結婚等は吉。葬式は凶
閉 とづ 閉じ込める日 金銭出納・建墓は吉。棟上げ・結婚・開店は凶
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} [[category:暦注|しゆうにちよく]] [[Category:名数12|ちよく]]

{| class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="2" | 十二直 ! 意味 ! 運勢 |- | 建 | たつ | 万物を建て生じる日 | よろず大吉の日。但し動土・蔵開きは凶 |- | 除 | のぞく | 障害を取り除く日 | 井戸掘り・治療開始・祭祀などは吉。結婚・動土は凶 |- | 満 | みつ | 全てが満たされる日 | 新規事・移転・結婚などは吉。動土・服薬は凶 |- | 平 | たいら | 物事が平らかになる日 | 旅行・結婚・道路修理などは吉。穴掘り・種まきは凶 |- | 定 | さだん | 善悪が定まる日 | 開店・結婚・移転・種まきは吉。旅行・訴訟は凶 |- | 執 | とる | 執り行う日 | 祭祀・祝い事・造作・種まきは吉。金銭の出入りは凶 |- | 破 | やぶる | 物事を突破する日 | 訴訟・出陣・漁猟・服薬は吉。祝い事・契約事は凶 |- | 危 | あやぶ | 物事を危惧する日 | 万事控えめに |- | 成 | なる | 物事が成就する日 | 新規事・建築・開店は吉。訴訟・談判は凶 |- | 納 | おさん | 物事を納め入れる日 | 収穫・商品購入は吉。結婚・見合いは凶 |- | 開 | ひらく | 開き通じる日 | 建築・移転・結婚等は吉。葬式は凶 |- | 閉 | とづ | 閉じ込める日 | 金銭出納・建墓は吉。棟上げ・結婚・開店は凶 |}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうにちよく}} [[category:暦注]] [[Category:名数12|ちよく]] [[Category:中国の民間信仰]]

'''十二直'''(じゅうにちょく)とは[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。
暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。「直」には「当たる」という意味があり、よく当たる暦注だと信じられていたと考えられる。
==由来== [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを吉凶判断に用いたのが'''十二直'''である。
昭和初期までは、'''十二直'''が暦注の中でも最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている。<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm 十二直について]</ref>
==配当== 柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分(斗柄)が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。
十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月ごとに、その月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には、節月の始まりの日に、その前の日の十二直を繰り返す。
冬至の頃には斗柄が北(子)を指す(建(おざ)す)ので、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。
==運勢== それぞれの十二直の意味と、一般的に言われているその日の運勢を示す。なお、最近の運勢暦では、十二直はひらがなで書かれることが多い。

'''十二直'''（じゅうにちょく）は、[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。

昭和初期までは、'''十二直'''が暦注の中でも最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm 十二直について]</ref>。

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうにちよく}} [[Category:暦注]] [[Category:中国の名数12|ちよく]] [[Category:中国の民間信仰]]

'''十二直'''（じゅうにちょく）は、[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを十二支に一致させて吉凶判断に用いたのが十二直である<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref>。昭和初期までは十二直が暦注中で最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm 十二直について]</ref>。
柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分（斗柄）が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月毎にその月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には節月の最初の日にその前の日の十二直を繰り返す。冬至の頃には斗柄が北（子）を指す（健す）ことから、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。
'''ただし[[四柱推命]]などの判断と相反・矛盾する内容が所々で見受けられ'''、四柱推命では完全な凶相となっている十二支の組み合わせが、十二直では吉凶半々となっていたりしている。例えば四柱推命では「辰・辰」「午・午」「酉・酉」「亥・亥」の組み合わせは「自刑」となり凶相となるが、十二直ではこれら4個の組み合わせは「建」となり、吉相を含んだ判断となる。
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |十二直一覧 |- ! colspan="2" | 十二直 ! 意味 ! 詳細 |- | 建 | たつ | 万物を建て生じる日 | 大半において吉。蔵開き・動土は凶。 |- | 除 | のぞく | 障害を取り除く日 | 井戸掘り・祭祀・治療開始は吉。結婚と動土は凶。 |- | 満 | みつ | 全てが満たされる日 | 移転・結婚・新規事は吉。動土と服薬は凶。 |- | 平 | たいら | 物事が平らかになる日 | 結婚・道路修理・旅行は吉。穴掘りと種蒔きは凶。 |- | 定 | さだん | 善悪が定まる日 | 移転・開店・結婚・種蒔きは吉。訴訟と旅行は凶。 |- | 執 | とる | 執り行う日 | 祭祀・祝祭・造作・種蒔きは吉。金銭出納は凶。 |- | 破 | やぶる | 物事を突破する日 | 出陣・訴訟・服薬・漁猟は吉。契約と祝祭は凶。 |- | 危 | あやぶ | 物事を危惧する日 | 全てにおいて凶。自粛が吉。 |- | 成 | なる | 物事が成就する日 | 開店・建築・新規事は吉。訴訟は凶。 |- | 納 | おさん | 物事を納め入れる日 | 購入と収穫は吉。結婚は凶。 |- | 開 | ひらく | 開き通じる日 | 移転・結婚・建築は吉。葬式は凶。 |- | 閉 | とづ | 閉じ込める日 | 金銭収納と建墓は吉。開店・結婚・棟上げは凶。 |} <ref name=":0" /> {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |十二支の組み合わせ一覧 |- ! ! 建 !除 !満 !平 !定 !執 !破 !危 !成 !納 !開 !閉 |- !子月 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |- !丑月 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |- ! 寅月 | 寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |- !卯月 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |- !辰月 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |- !巳月 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |- !午月 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |- !未月 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |- !申月 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |- !酉月 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |- !戌月 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |- !亥月 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |}

柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分（斗柄）が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月毎にその月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には節月の最初の日にその前の日の十二直を繰り返す。冬至の頃には斗柄が北（子）を指す（健す）ことから、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。
'''ただし[[四柱推命]]などの判断と相反・矛盾する内容が所々で見受けられ'''、四柱推命では完全な凶相となっている十二支の組み合わせが、十二直では吉凶半々となっていたりしている。例えば四柱推命では「辰・辰」「午・午」「酉・酉」「亥・亥」の組み合わせは「自刑」となり凶相となるが、十二直ではこれら4個の組み合わせは「建」となり、吉相を含んだ判断となる。
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |十二直一覧 |- ! colspan="2" | 十二直 ! 意味 ! 詳細 |- | 建 | たつ | 万物を建て生じる日 | 大半が吉。蔵開き・動土は凶。 |- | 除 | のぞく | 障害を取り除く日 | 井戸掘り・祭祀・治療開始は吉。結婚と動土は凶。 |- | 満 | みつ | 全てが満たされる日 | 移転・結婚・新規事は吉。動土と服薬は凶。 |- | 平 | たいら | 物事が平らかになる日 | 結婚・道路修理・旅行は吉。穴掘りと種蒔きは凶。 |- | 定 | さだん | 善悪が定まる日 | 移転・開店・結婚・種蒔きは吉。訴訟と旅行は凶。 |- | 執 | とる | 執り行う日 | 祭祀・祝祭・造作・種蒔きは吉。金銭出納は凶。 |- | 破 | やぶる | 物事を突破する日 | 出陣・訴訟・服薬・漁猟は吉。契約と祝祭は凶。 |- | 危 | あやぶ | 物事を危惧する日 | 全て凶。自粛が吉。 |- | 成 | なる | 物事が成就する日 | 開店・建築・新規事は吉。訴訟は凶。 |- | 納 | おさん | 物事を納め入れる日 | 購入と収穫は吉。結婚は凶。 |- | 開 | ひらく | 開き通じる日 | 移転・結婚・建築は吉。葬式は凶。 |- | 閉 | とづ | 閉じ込める日 | 金銭収納と建墓は吉。開店・結婚・棟上げは凶。 |} <ref name=":0" /> {| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |十二支の組み合わせ一覧 |- ! ! 建 !除 !満 !平 !定 !執 !破 !危 !成 !納 !開 !閉 |- !子月 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |- !丑月 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |- ! 寅月 | 寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |- !卯月 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |- !辰月 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |- !巳月 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |- !午月 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |- !未月 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |- !申月 |申 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |- !酉月 |酉 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |- !戌月 |戌 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |- !亥月 |亥 |子 |丑 |寅 |卯 |辰 |巳 |午 |未 |申 |酉 |戌 |}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しゆうにちよく}} [[Category:名数12|ちよく]] [[Category:中国の名数12|ちよく]] [[Category:天体]] [[Category:天文学]] [[Category:天文学史]] [[Category:天文学に関する記事]] [[Category:中国天文学]] [[Category:月]] [[Category:太陽]] [[Category:暦法]] [[Category:太陰暦]] [[Category:太陰太陽暦]] [[Category:太陽暦]] [[Category:農事暦]] [[Category:節気]] [[Category:曜日]] [[Category:中国の無形文化遺産]] [[Category:占星術]] [[Category:占い]] [[Category:術数学]] [[Category:陰陽道]] [[Category:暦注]] [[Category:中国の民間信仰]]

'''十二直'''（じゅうにちょく）は、[[暦注]]の一つで、'''建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉'''のことである。暦の中段に記載されているため、「中段」「中段十二直」とも呼ばれる。
== 概要 == [[北斗七星]]は古代から畏敬の念を持って見られた星座の一つであるが、この星の動きを十二支に一致させて吉凶判断に用いたのが十二直である<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref>。昭和初期までは十二直が暦注中で最重視されていたが、最近では[[六曜]]や[[九星]]を重視する人が多くなり、以前ほどは使われなくなっている<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm 十二直について]</ref>。
柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分（斗柄）が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月毎にその月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には節月の最初の日にその前の日の十二直を繰り返す。冬至の頃には斗柄が北（子）を指す（健す）ことから、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。
'''ただし[[四柱推命]]などの判断と相反・矛盾する内容が所々で見受けられ'''、四柱推命では完全な凶相となっている十二支の組み合わせが、十二直では吉凶半々となっていたりしており、不可解な点が多い。例えば四柱推命では「辰・辰」「午・午」「酉・酉」「亥・亥」の組み合わせは「自刑」となり凶相となるが、十二直ではこれら4個の組み合わせは「建」となり、吉相を含んだ判断となる。

柄杓の形をした北斗七星の柄に当たる部分（斗柄）が[[北極星]]を中心にして天球上を回転することから、これに[[十二支]]による方位と組み合せて十二直を配当する。十二直に用いる月は[[節月]]である。節月毎にその月の夕刻に斗柄が向いている方位の十二支と、日の十二支とが同じになる日が「建」になるように配当する。実際には節月の最初の日にその前の日の十二直を繰り返す。冬至の頃には斗柄が北（子）を指す（健す）ことから、冬至を含む月を「建子の月」という。

revision.text:
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions|ja|二十八宿}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Terms|ja|節月}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[category:unsorted completion categories]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions|ja|二十八宿}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:十二直]]

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:defautsort]]

For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing pages to myself
revision.text:
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:defautsort]]
[[category:unsorted completion categories]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! Rat Month || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig |- ! Dragon Month || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit |- ! Snake Month || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon |- ! Horse Month || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake |- ! Goat Month || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse |- ! Monkey Month || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat |- ! Rooster Month || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey |- ! Dog Month || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster |- ! Pig Month || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog |}

revision.comment: adding to [[Category:Oldest 50 pages as of February 16 2025]]
revision.text:
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]]
[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! Rooster Month || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey |- ! Dog Month || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster |- ! revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Oldest 50 pages as of February 16 2025]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! Monkey Month || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat |- ! Rooster Month || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey |- ! '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

1-25]] [[ja:十二直]] [[Category:Oldest 50 pages as of February 16 2025]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]]

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

1-25]] [[ja:十二直]] [[Category:Oldest 50 pages as of February 16 2025]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Calendrical Notes|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|ja|二十八宿|Twenty-Eight Mansions=Twenty-Eight Mansions|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|ja|北斗七星}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} and {{ill|Nine Celestial Stems|ja|九星}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar Term|ja|節月|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Twelve Directions-qq]] However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|ja|四柱推命}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing.
