The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. revision.comment: Add page with conflicting Wikidata QIDs
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh_yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh_yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh_yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh_yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].

Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

{{wikidata link|Q11404810}} [[Category:Agricultural calendar]] [[Category:Arithmetic and geometry]] [[Category:Articles about astronomy]] [[Category:Astrology]] [[Category:Astronomy]] [[Category:Astronomy in China]] [[Category:Calendars]] [[Category:Chinese astronomy]] [[Category:Chinese Enumerations of 12]] [[Category:Chinese folk religion]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Days of the week]] [[Category:Divination]] [[Category:Enumerations of 12]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of astronomy]] [[Category:Lunar calendars]] [[Category:Lunisolar calendars]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:rekichū]] [[Category:Solar calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or [[:en:Twenty-Eight Mansions|Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions]].
== Overview ==
The [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs [[:en:Solar term|Solar Terms]] to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like [[:en:Four Pillars of Destiny|Four Pillars of Destiny]], where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11404810]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|en|Four Pillars of Destiny|WD=Q843288|lt=Four Pillars of Destiny}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! Rat Month || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig |- ! Ox Month || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat |- ! Tiger Month || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox |- ! Rabbit Month || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger |- ! Dragon Month || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit |- ! Snake Month || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon |- ! Horse Month || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake |- ! Goat Month || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse |- ! Monkey Month || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat |- ! Rooster Month || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey |- ! Dog Month || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster |- ! Pig Month || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog |}

However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|en|Four Pillars of Destiny|WD=Q843288|lt=Four Pillars of Destiny}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]]
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=wall calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar Terms}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|en|Four Pillars of Destiny|WD=Q843288|lt=Four Pillars of Destiny}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:十二直]][[zh-yue:建除]][[zh:建除十二神]]
'''Jūnichoku''' (十二直) is one of the {{ill|Rekichū|lt=calendar annotations|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Calendrical Notes|qq=}}, comprising twelve categories: '''Ken, Jo, Mitsu, Taira, Sadamu, Toru, Yaburu, Ayabu, Naru, Osamu, Hiraku, and Tozuru'''. These are often recorded in the middle section of calendars, leading to the alternative name "Middle Section" (中段) or "Middle Section Twelve Categories" (中段十二直). They are frequently used alongside systems like the {{ill|Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|ja|二十七宿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twenty-Seven Lunar Mansions|1=Q11371366|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11371366}} or {{ill|Twenty-Eight Mansions|en|Twenty-Eight Mansions|WD=Q1378760|lt=Twenty-Eight Lunar Mansions|1=Twenty-Eight Mansions}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac|1=Earthly Branches}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=Calendar (publication)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar Terms|1=Solar term}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|en|Four Pillars of Destiny|WD=Q843288|lt=Four Pillars of Destiny|1=Four Pillars of Destiny}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="4" |List of Jūnichoku<ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=暦の吉凶 十二直 {{!}} 歳事暦 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220925225350/https://saijigoyomi.com/12choku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-09-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|title=十二直（建・除・満・平・定・執・破・危・成・納・開・閉）の由来や意味、読み方とは？ - 日本文化研究ブログ - Japan Culture Lab |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625022436/https://jpnculture.net/juunichoku/ |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-06-25 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> |- ! colspan="2" | Jūnichoku ! Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
{| class="wikitable" ! colspan="13" |Twelve Zodiac Combinations<ref name=":0" /> |- ! !! Ken !! Jo !! Mitsu !! Taira !! Sadamu !! Toru !! Yaburu !! Ayabu !! Naru !! Osamu !! Hiraku !! Tozuru |- ! Rat Month || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig |- ! Ox Month || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat |- ! Tiger Month || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox |- ! Rabbit Month || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger |- ! Dragon Month || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit |- ! Snake Month || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon |- ! Horse Month || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake |- ! Goat Month || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse |- ! Monkey Month || Monkey || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat |- ! Rooster Month || Rooster || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey |- ! Dog Month || Dog || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster |- ! Pig Month || Pig || Rat || Ox || Tiger || Rabbit || Dragon || Snake || Horse || Goat || Monkey || Rooster || Dog |}

== Overview ==
The {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}} has been regarded with reverence since ancient times, and the motion of this constellation was aligned with the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac|1=Earthly Branches}} to determine auspicious and inauspicious days, forming the basis of Jūnichoku.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=暦Wiki/十二直 - 国立天文台暦計算室 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220827083341/https://eco.mtk.nao.ac.jp/koyomi/wiki/BDBDC6F3C4BE.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2022-08-27 |access-date=2023-01-14}}</ref> Until the early {{ill|Shōwa period|ja|昭和時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 昭和|1=Shōwa era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q327567}}, Jūnichoku was the most emphasized system among calendar annotations. However, more recently, systems like {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} and {{ill|Nine Stars|ja|九星|1=Nine Stars|2=ja|3=九星|lt=Nine Stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nine Stars|qq=}} have gained prominence, and Jūnichoku is now less frequently used.<ref>[http://homepage1.nifty.com/gyouseinet/calendar/12tyoku.htm About Jūnichoku]</ref> It is also rarely included in {{ill|seven-day calendars|ja|七曜表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seven-day calendars|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カレンダー|1=Calendar (publication)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1309927}}.
The handle of the Big Dipper (斗柄) is believed to rotate around the {{ill|North Star|ja|北極星|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/North Star|1=North Star|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1211496}}, and its alignment with the directions of the twelve zodiac animals is used to determine Jūnichoku assignments. The system employs {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar Terms|1=Solar term}} to allocate categories. For each solar month, the day when the zodiac of the evening Dipper aligns with the day's zodiac is designated as "Ken." In practice, the first day of the solar month repeats the previous day’s category. For example, during the winter solstice, when the Dipper points north (Rat), the solar month is referred to as the "Ken-Zi Month." However, contradictions arise when comparing Jūnichoku with systems like {{ill|Four Pillars of Destiny|en|Four Pillars of Destiny|WD=Q843288|lt=Four Pillars of Destiny|1=Four Pillars of Destiny}}, where certain zodiac combinations considered completely inauspicious in Four Pillars are treated as mixed or even auspicious in Jūnichoku. For instance, combinations like "Dragon-Dragon" or "Rooster-Rooster" are considered self-punishments ("Jikei") in Four Pillars but are categorized as "Ken" in Jūnichoku, carrying favorable elements.

Meaning ! Details |- | Ken || たつ || A day to build and generate. || Auspicious: Most activities. Inauspicious: Storehouse opening, groundbreaking. |- | Jo || のぞく || A day to remove obstacles. || Auspicious: Well digging, rituals, medical treatments. Inauspicious: Marriage, groundbreaking. |- | Mitsu || みつ || A day when all is fulfilled. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Groundbreaking, taking medicine. |- | Taira || たいら || A day for smoothing things out. || Auspicious: Marriage, road repair, travel. Inauspicious: Digging, sowing. |- | Sadamu || さだん || A day when good and bad are determined. || Auspicious: Moving, opening stores, marriage, sowing. Inauspicious: Litigation, travel. |- | Toru || とる || A day to take and act. || Auspicious: Rituals, celebrations, construction, sowing. Inauspicious: Financial transactions. |- | Yaburu || やぶる || A day to break through obstacles. || Auspicious: Military expeditions, litigation, taking medicine, fishing. Inauspicious: Contracts, celebrations. |- | Ayabu || あやぶ || A day to fear danger. || Auspicious: Caution. Inauspicious: All activities. |- | Naru || なる || A day when things come to fruition. || Auspicious: Store openings, construction, new endeavors. Inauspicious: Litigation. |- | Osamu || おさん || A day to store or collect. || Auspicious: Purchases, harvests. Inauspicious: Marriage. |- | Hiraku || ひらく || A day to open and connect. || Auspicious: Moving, marriage, construction. Inauspicious: Funerals. |- | Tozuru || とづ || A day to enclose or restrict. || Auspicious: Financial collection, grave construction. Inauspicious: Store openings, marriage, raising beams. |}
